description: Diagnostic decision tree for A2779 (MacBook Pro 14" M2 Pro/Max, J414-C). Follow sequentially from Step 1. Has MagSafe, 3x USB-C (2 left, 1 right), HDMI, SD Card. Uses Austringer VR (replaces Viper) and CLVR VR (replaces Monaco).
WARNING: A2779 (820-02655) J414-C is the Pro/Max variant. NOT the standard J414-S (820-02841). Austringer VR replaces Viper for GPU/CPU cluster power. CLVR VR replaces Monaco for additional power domains. SVR AON is 3-phase with U5700 controller (higher current than base M2). MagSafe on pages 54-56.
data_source: Schematic PNGs cross-referenced with Eason reference.

eason_reference.source: M1 series standby circuit and power-on principle, and 20V boost circuit (Eason @ iphone_cpu_fix)
power_on_sequence: 1. PPVBUS_USBC 5V → CD3217 MOSFET → PPHV_INT_AONSW → fuse → PPDCIN_AONSW
2. PPDCIN_AONSW → charger IC U5200 detect and power inputs (page 52)
3. U5200 → gate drives → PPBUS_AON (12.6V temp). Charger support on page 53
4. CHGR_EN → P3V8AON_PWR_EN → U5700 SVR AON controller (3-phase)
6. PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN → AON throttle page 60 → U8100 (MPMU) comparator. When voltage detect passes, PMU_VDD_HI → U8100 starts
7. U8100 MPMU → all MPMU rails (pages 81-83). U7700 SPMU → all SPMU rails (pages 77-79)
8. M2 Pro/Max hard start: 24MHz crystal + 32.768KHz crystal + PMU_RESET_L. SoC reads ROM, I2C to CD3217
9. SoC → MPMU → P5VS2_PWR_EN → PP5V_S2 (page 122). Austringer VR enables (pages 92-96). CLVR VR enables (pages 101-105, 120)
10. CD3217 reads shared ROM, PD negotiation via CC → charger boosts to 20V → real PPBUS_AON

forced_start_bypass.description: Bypass protective isolation to test if fault is pre-SVR (front end) or post-SVR (back end).
steps: 1. Connect PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN to 3.8V externally (4.3V with display)
2. Force PMU_VDD_HI high at comparator output on AON throttle page (page 60)
3. Press start button OR connect charger for 3 seconds

interpretation.forced_start_works: Fault is FRONT END — CD3217, charger IC, fuses, MagSafe path, boost circuit. Check Steps 1-2.
interpretation.forced_start_fails: Fault is BACK END — PMU, SoC, clocks, ROM, Austringer/CLVR VR. Check Steps 3+.

ppvaon_fault_l.description: Check PPVAON_FAULT_L on SVR AON page (page 57). If low, U5700 reports fault. Check phase drivers and output caps.

pmu_active_ready.description: Check PMU_ACTIVE_READY from MPMU U8100 (page 83). If never asserts, MPMU did not complete startup.

crystal_32khz.description: Check 32.768KHz crystal for MPMU. If missing or shorted, MPMU will not start.

soc_force_dfu.description: If all power present but no boot, force DFU mode via USB-C to test SoC/ROM.

phase_balance.description: Check SVR AON 3-phase balance. Thermal cam for uneven heating across phase drivers.

cd3217_sys_detect.description: Check SYS_DETECT from battery connector. If missing, charger IC will not enable.

pp5v_s2_bootstrap.description: PP5V_S2 enables AFTER SoC hard start (page 122). If missing, SoC may not have started.

austringer_fault.description: Check Austringer VR for GPU/CPU cluster power faults. Replaces Viper on M2 Pro/Max. Multiple power stages.
austringer_fault.probe: Austringer controller and power stages, pages 92-96

clvr_fault.description: Check CLVR VR for additional power domain faults. Replaces Monaco on M2 Pro/Max.
clvr_fault.probe: CLVR instances, pages 101-105 and throttle page 120

tree.probe: Bulk capacitors near U5200 charger IC output (page 52). Charger support page 53.

check_sequence: Check PPDCIN_AON (charger input, page 52)
Check SYS_DETECT signal from battery connector
Check CD3217 port controllers for all 3 USB-C ports: Left ATC0 (page 72), ATC1 (page 76), Right ATC2 (page 82)
Check charger IC U5200 area gate drives (page 52)
Check charger support components (page 53)
Check MagSafe path: connector and control (pages 54-56)
Check dual inductors and sense resistors (page 52)
Thermal cam on charger area for shorted FETs

tree.probe: SVR AON output caps near U5700 (pages 57-58). 3-phase output. AON throttle page 60.

no.diagnosis: SVR AON (U5700) fault — 3-phase, higher current design
check_sequence: Check P3V8AON_PWR_EN enable signal (page 57)
Check P3V8AON_FAULT_L (active low fault, page 57)
Check 3-phase output and ISENSE summing (page 58)
Check AON throttle circuit (page 60) — PMU_VDD_HI comparator
Check phase drivers and inductors for all 3 phases
Thermal cam on U5700 and phase drivers — higher current than base M2
SHORTCUT: Try Eason forced start bypass — inject 3.8V on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN + force PMU_VDD_HI high on page 60 + press start. If board boots, fault is upstream of SVR.

tree.probe: External AON LDO outputs. Fed from PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN.

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON input to LDOs — if missing, fault is upstream at U5700
If PP3V3_AON missing: check LDO regulator input/output, diode mode on output cap
If PP1V8_AON missing: check LDO regulator input/output, diode mode on output cap
If PP1V2_AON missing: check LDO regulator input/output, diode mode on output cap
Thermal cam on LDO area

tree.probe: Slave PMU buck output caps (pages 77-79). VIN page 77, LDO/Bucks page 78, GPIO page 79.
tree.expect: Multiple SPMU buck rails present — DRAM, NAND, SoC sleep domains

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON input to U7700 (SPMU VIN, page 77)
Check PPBUS_AON input to SPMU (page 77)
Check SPMU LDO outputs (page 78)
Check SPMU BUCK outputs (page 78)
Check SPMU GPIO signals including SPMU_READY, SPMU_FAULT (page 79)
Diode mode across output cap banks for each buck
Thermal cam on U7700 area for hot component
If single buck missing: find downstream short on that rail before replacing PMU

tree.probe: Master PMU buck output caps (pages 81-83). VIN page 81, LDO/Bucks page 82, GPIO page 83.
tree.expect: Multiple MPMU buck rails present — PCPU, GPU, SOC, fabric domains

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_MPMU input to U8100 (MPMU VIN, page 81)
Check PPBUS_AON_MPMU input (page 81)
CRITICAL: Check comparator on AON throttle page (page 60) — PMU_VDD_HI must go high for U8100 to start
Check 32.768KHz crystal — MPMU needs this clock
Check PMU_ONOFF_L signal
Check PMU_ACTIVE_READY output (page 83)
Check PMU_FAULT_INT_L (page 83)
Check MPMU LDO outputs (page 82)
Check MPMU BUCK outputs (page 82)
Check MPMU GPIO signals (page 83)
Check PMU support components (pages 84-85)
Thermal cam on U8100 and surrounding area

no.diagnosis: PP5V_S2 regulator fault — enables AFTER SoC hard start
check_sequence: Check P5V2TPS_PWR_EN enable signal (page 122)
Check PPBUS_AON input to TPS62130
Check output inductor and sense resistor
PP5V_S2 enables AFTER SoC hard start — if missing, SoC may not have started. Verify SoC clocks and ROM first.
Diode mode on output caps for shorts

tree.check: Austringer VR — GPU/CPU cluster power (replaces Viper)
tree.expect: Variable voltage present (DVFS) on CPU/GPU cluster domains

no.diagnosis: Austringer VR fault — GPU/CPU cluster power
check_sequence: Check Austringer controller enable signals (page 92)
Check Austringer fault output — AUSTRINGER_FAULT_L (page 92)
Check power stage 1 (page 93) — inductors, sense resistors, FETs
Check power stage 2 (page 94)
Check power stage 3 (page 95)
Check Austringer support / feedback (page 96)
Check input cap banks on each power stage
Thermal cam on Austringer controller and power stage MOSFETs
Max variant: additional stages may be Max-specific — check if only Max-specific stage fails

tree.check: CLVR VR — additional power domains (replaces Monaco)
tree.expect: Variable voltage present (DVFS) on fabric/additional GPU domains
tree.yes: SoC power domains fed. Fault is downstream. Go to Step 9.

check_sequence: Check which CLVR instance is failing (pages 101-105)
Check CLVR GPIO connections for ALERT signals on each instance
Check CLVR fabric throttle (page 120)
Check CLVR input caps on each instance
Max variant: additional CLVR instances are Max-specific — check if only Max-specific instance fails
Thermal cam on CLVR controllers

tree.expect: Multiple voltage domains present. Board should attempt boot.

no.diagnosis: SoC fault, SPI ROM fault, or clock issue
check_sequence: Check SPI NOR flash
Check secure element
Check SoC ground connections
Check 24MHz crystal oscillator
Check 32.768KHz crystal
If all power present but no boot: try DFU mode via USB-C
PMU output desense check (page 76) — verify desense filtering is not masking a fault

substeps.signals: CD3217 port controllers, VBUS switches, CC lines, SBU lines
CD3217 port controller, VBUS switch, CC lines, SBU lines

substeps.probe: SD card controller GL9755 and LDOs (pages 96-99)
MagSafe connector, control IC, and protection (pages 54-56)
SSD0 controller and NAND (pages 102-107), SSD1 controller and NAND (pages 108-112)
Keyboard controller, membrane connector, ESD protection

substeps.note: Module is soldered — rework requires BGA skills
